macro_segments.title: Revisiting Chazakah and Field Boundaries: The Role of Physical Demarcations

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Daf: The Question of What Demarcates Separate Fields for Chazakah
Previous Day's Discussion: Pe'ah, Tum'ah, and Whether Boundary Markers Create One Field or Two
Shabbat Application: Rava's View that Boundary Markers Separate Domains for Shabbat

macro_segments.title: Shabbat Domains and Public Domain Separation: Rava and Abaye's Dispute Over What Constitutes Separate Reshuyot

micro_segments.title: The Baraita on Carrying Half a Pressed Fig: When Multiple Acts Combine Under One Uninterrupted State of Forgetting
Rabbi Yosi's Requirement: Acts Must Be in One Domain (Reshut Achas) to Combine
Rava's Definition: Private Domain Between Two Public Domains as the Threshold for Separation
Abaye's Counterposition: Even a Karmelit (Non-Halachic Domain) Can Separate Public Domains
Rava's Response: Even a Pisla (Raised Platform) Separates Domains for Shabbat Purposes
Rava's Consistency: Reshut Shabbat Functions Similarly to Reshut Gittin in Demarcating Space

macro_segments.title: The Plant Chatzav (Sea Squill): Biblical Boundary Marker and Its Characteristics

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's Question: What Defines Field Boundaries in the Absence of Physical Markers
Rashi and Rashbam's Interpretation: Recognition by Community as the Defining Factor
Tosafot's Alternative: Returning to the Earlier Tum'ah Question of Field Distinction
Identification of Chatzav: Sea Squill Plant with Straight Roots and Annual Growth Pattern
Joshua's Use of Chatzav: Demarcating Tribal and Family Land Divisions in Eretz Yisrael

macro_segments.title: Digression on Eretz Yisrael Boundaries: Kedusha Rishona, the Promised Land, and the Lands of Keini, Kenizi, and Kadmoni

micro_segments.title: Why Joshua Only Listed Border Cities: Establishing the Framework for Understanding Eretz Yisrael Sanctity
Rav Yehuda's Statement on Terumot and Ma'aser: Divine Promise vs. Actual Conquest as the Defining Criterion
The Three Extra Nations Promised to Abraham: Why Keini, Kenizi, and Kadmoni Are Excluded from Kedusha
Rashbam's View: Lands Too Distant Ever Become Incorporated into Eretz Yisrael Proper Despite Future Conquest
Tosafot's Taming: Kedusha Rishona Defined by Conquest, Not Promise; Border Reality Matters More Than Divine Covenant
Three Rabbinic Opinions on Keini, Kenizi, Kadmoni: Identification and Geographic Boundaries of Excluded Lands

macro_segments.title: The Mishnah on False Witnesses and Chazakah: How Multiple Groups of Witnesses Across Three Years Combine

micro_segments.title: The Basic Mishnah: Two Witnesses Per Year Over Three Years; If All Lie, They Divide Liability Among Themselves
Brothers Testifying on Separate Years: Avoiding the Prohibition on Multiple Brothers by Spreading Their Testimony

macro_segments.title: Rava's Position Versus Rabbi Akiva: Do Three Separate Years of Testimony Combine to Establish Chazakah

micro_segments.title: The Tannaitic Dispute: Rabbi Akiva Demands Full Testimony to Each Davar; Rabbanan Accept Separate Yearly Witnesses
Rabbi Akiva's Stringency: Any Single Year Is Not Full Testimony; Partial Matters Cannot Stand Independently
Rabbanan's Response: What Constitutes a Chatzi Davar (Half Matter) That Cannot Combine
Rashabm and Tosafot on Chatzi Davar: Information vs. Independent Meaning as the Criterion

macro_segments.title: Wheat Versus Barley Testimony: When Contradictory Witness Accounts Can Still Establish Chazakah

micro_segments.title: The Question: One Witness Says Wheat, Another Says Barley for the Same Three Years—Does It Combine?
Rav Nachman's Challenge: Non-Consecutive Years (1,3,5 vs. 2,4,6)—Can They Combine If Gaps Are Customary Fallow?
The Answer: Minor Distinctions Don't Count as Contradiction; Witnesses Are Imprecise but Consistent in Intent
The Unresolved Question: Can Testimony for Consecutive Years 1-3 Plus 4-6 Combine Without Explicit Connection?
Rashbam's Research: Actual Farmer Practice Shows Crop Rotation (Wheat-Barley Alternation) Without Fallow Years

topical_tags.term: kedusha rishona (first sanctification of Eretz Yisrael)
Keini, Kenizi, ve-Kadmoni (three nations promised to Abraham but excluded)
Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabbanan (dispute on combining yearly testimonies)
he'elam echad (one continuous state of forgetting on Shabbat)
tokh kedei akhilat pras (the time it takes to eat half a loaf)
gittin (bills of divorce; relevance to space demarcation)
kibbush rabbim (conquest by community; public conquest)
Rava (major Amora; boundary and domain distinctions)
Abaye (Rava's contemporary; more lenient on domain separation)
terumot and ma'aser (priestly gifts and tithes)
Esau and Lot (descendants of Abraham's family receiving promised lands)
